title: blazewrit harness — decide step structured_output
description: Decide = the OWNER of the decision, sitting on Investigate output (every flow runs Decide, no skip). Output depth is declared via a per-flow MODE: record | plan | design. TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation (M1 = SHAPE). This schema models the Decide OUTPUT as a top-level discriminated union on `result`: a normal `decision` (which itself carries the 3-mode record|plan|design sub-union), the two prose-mandated control outputs `reclassify_required` (triage_mismatch surfaced) and `request_upstream_deepen` (shallow Ground/Investigate, cap 1), plus the precondition-fault output `precondition_fault` (a required-but-missing/malformed upstream field detected by the Input-preconditions gate; Decide does NOT decide, it escalates with failure_origin=investigate|ground). $ref-s ./_defs.schema.json for all shared primitives (M4 single source). IMPORTANT: grammar here enforces SHAPE only. The MODE is FORCED MECHANICALLY by R6 from Investigate schema fields (NOT free-chosen by the LLM) — the orchestrator computes the forced mode and a SIBLING M2 validator_contract (see x-validator-contracts) asserts the emitted `mode` matches that R6 computation. Other truth-bearing fields (R6 mode-force, R16 chain, R20 verify probes in plans, R23 measured counts, R26 provenance, R36 citations, R5 cross-verify on Design) are likewise closed by deterministic code checks listed in x-validator-contracts, NOT by any keyword here.
$comment: NO root-level additionalProperties:false — JSON Schema additionalProperties only sees SIBLING properties, not properties introduced inside the oneOf branches. Closure is enforced per-branch (each oneOf member sets additionalProperties:false + lists its full property set). The root `properties.result` below is advisory (gives `result` a description); the binding discriminant + closure live in each branch.

result.$comment: Top-level discriminant. Grammar forces exactly one branch via the oneOf below.
result.description: Which Decide output this is. 'decision' = a real decision was made (carries `mode` sub-union). 'reclassify_required' = Investigate surfaced triage_mismatch; Decide does NOT proceed, signals reclassify (orchestrator re-enters Triage; cap 3/flow). 'request_upstream_deepen' = Ground/Investigate too shallow to decide; Decide signals upstream deepen (cap 1/flow, then Verify is final safety). 'precondition_fault' = the Input-preconditions gate found a required upstream field absent/malformed/enum-invalid/ref-unresolved; Decide does NOT decide, it escalates with failure_origin=investigate|ground (5-累積 halt cap bounds re-entry). 'self_misjudgment' = Design cross-verify ran and CONFLICTED (pyreez cross_verify_result==disagree); Decide does NOT emit a decision, it escalates with failure_origin=verify (the self-misjudgment signal — a `decision` carrying disagree is forbidden, it must take this branch).

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH A — a real decision. Carries the 3-mode (record|plan|design) sub-union.

mode.$comment: R6 — the forced mode discriminant. Grammar enforces the value is one of the three AND (via the nested oneOf below) that the per-mode required fields are present. TRUTH (that this value == the R6-computed forced mode) is an M2 validator_contract, not a keyword.
mode.description: Decide mode = output depth. record (self-evident decision, depth=shallow) | plan (option selection, depth=deep) | design (full design, depth=deep). FORCED MECHANICALLY by R6 from Investigate fields — the orchestrator computes it; the LLM enters with it pre-forced. The nested oneOf on this same `mode` value forces exactly one per-mode payload.

decision_record.$comment: Reviewer rule: ALL modes require decision_record + reason. One-line authoritative statement of the decision taken.
decision_record.description: The decision itself, as one authoritative statement. REQUIRED in every mode (decide-reviewer: 'decision_record + reason 필수' for all modes).

reason.$comment: ALL modes require reason. The justification, grounded in Ground/Investigate facts.
reason.description: Why this decision — grounded in Ground/Investigate FACTS (reviewer: 'ground·investigate 사실에 근거'). REQUIRED in every mode. Carries provenance: source_tool names which Ground/Investigate origin the reason rests on (R26 floor).

grounded_in.description: RowRefs to the specific Ground/Investigate rows whose facts justify this decision (§5: Postgres row ids, never file paths). At least one — a decision not grounded in any fact is rejected (reviewer: 'reason.grounded_in 실 row 해소').

based_on.$comment: §5 — based_on = RowRefs (Postgres rows), NEVER file paths. Legacy prose 'based_on: { investigate_ref, ground_ref }' translated to RowRef bundle.
based_on.description: Upstream rows this decision rests on. EVERY value is a RowRef (Postgres step_runs row id), never a file path. Decide always rests on Investigate and Ground; both are required here.

depth.$comment: Decide mode IS depth (Record=shallow / Plan/Design=deep per _defs.Depth). x-validator: depth must agree with mode. DELIBERATELY NOT const-pinned per mode sub-branch (unlike `mode`, which the nested oneOf const-pins, and unlike the plan/design-only props the sub-branches forbid with false): the mode<->depth coupling (record=>shallow, plan/design=>deep) is enforced by the M2 validator R6/mode-depth, NOT by grammar here. Reason: depth is also the carrier for the R13/R16 chain shape and is read uniformly across branches; pinning it would split the property into the nested oneOf and duplicate the mode discriminant. The grammar therefore admits a shape-legal mode/depth pairing that the M2 R6/mode-depth check rejects — a documented M1-shape/M2-truth split, mirroring how `cross_verify_result==disagree` is shape-legal but M2-rejected on a `decision` output.
depth.description: Adaptive Step Depth this Decide ran at. Decide maps mode→depth (Record=shallow; Plan/Design=deep). M2 validator asserts depth agrees with `mode`.

declared_next_step.$comment: R16 — producer DECLARES, orchestrator INJECTS expected_next_step. Chain match is M2 (folded into R27 validator).
declared_next_step.description: R16 advisory next step. Decide does NOT free-fill the authoritative successor; the orchestrator injects expected_next_step. M2 validator asserts declared_next_step == expected_next_step (chain divergence check). Carries the shared DeclaredNextStep shape.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor — KEEP polarity. A Decide claim the step could not itself verify; designed to persist to Verify (the single gate). No 'no unverified remain' forbid.
unverified.description: R13 UNVERIFIED FLAG (irreducible floor, KEEP polarity). true = some part of this decision could not be verified by Decide and MUST propagate downstream to Verify. Never forbidden true at this intermediate step.

followup_flows.$comment: Available in ALL modes (record|plan|design) — generalized output slot. Two sources: Record audit surfacing AND partial_proceed.blocked_set queuing (any mode, esp. Plan). dedup by (type, scope_hash) is an M2 validator, not a grammar keyword.
followup_flows.description: Followup-flow queue slot, available in ALL three modes. Two sources, DISAMBIGUATED by the required `source` tag: (1) source=audit — Record/audit surfacing — Review Decide.Record surfaces review findings here; (2) source=blocked_scope — partial_proceed.blocked_set queuing — in ANY mode (esp. Plan: Migration-style partial_proceed flows run in Plan mode), Decide queues each blocked scope here. Orchestrator QUEUEs them, never auto-runs. M2 validator: dedup by (type, scope_hash). partial-proceed-coverage join counts ONLY source=blocked_scope entries (so an audit entry that happens to share a scope_hash can neither spuriously satisfy coverage nor conflate a real blocked scope). scope is a RowRef to the finding/blocked-scope row.

source.description: Which of the two queuing sources produced this entry. 'audit' = Review/audit surfacing (Record mode). 'blocked_scope' = partial_proceed.blocked_set queuing. The partial-proceed-coverage join counts ONLY blocked_scope entries; audit entries are never matched against blocked_set, so a scope_hash collision across the two sources cannot conflate them.

scope_ref.description: RowRef to the finding/work row this followup addresses (§5 row id, not a file path).

scope_hash.description: Stable hash of the followup scope. M2 validator dedups followup_flows by (type, scope_hash).

options_considered.description: The compared options. Reviewer demands N>=2 (an option set of one is not a plan). Each has an id, approach, trade_offs, and est_effort.

est_effort.description: Relative effort estimate for this option.

verify_probe.$comment: R20 — optional re-runnable assertion attached to an option. Truth is the RE-EXECUTION (M2), not the shape.
verify_probe.description: Optional R20 probe: a command + expected result that would validate this option's premise. M2 validator RE-EXECUTES and compares — self-assertion is not truth.

chosen.description: The selected option + why. option_id MUST reference an id present in options_considered (M2 validator).

sequencing.$comment: Compound sub-flow order / migration cycle order. depends_on values reference step ids within this sequence (M2 acyclicity/existence check).
sequencing.description: Optional priority/dependency ordering (Compound sub-flow order, Migration cycle order). Each entry: a step id + the step ids it depends on. M2 validator: every depends_on id exists and the graph is acyclic.

options_deliberated.description: pyreez deliberation record (option comparison feeding the architecture choice). pyreez is MCP-backed and degradable: when pyreez is unavailable this is the Omitted branch (R22), not an empty array or a fabricated deliberation.

value.$comment: minItems:2 — a MEASURED deliberation (pyreez RAN) must have deliberated at least two options; a measured deliberation of zero/one option is degenerate (deliberating nothing). The legitimate absence case is the Omitted branch, not an empty measured array.

design_document.$comment: Reviewer: design document MUST contain architecture + policy + userflow + req. All four required.
design_document.description: The design document. Reviewer requires all four sub-parts present: architecture, policy(/policies), userflow(s), requirements.

architecture.description: The chosen architecture decision (chosen_architecture in legacy prose).

policies.description: Policy / business rules: [{ rule, scope, exceptions }].

intent_card.$comment: Reviewer: Design must CREATE an emberdeck intent card. emberdeck is MCP-backed and degradable: card creation can fail when ED is unavailable → Omitted branch (R22), not a fake card id.
intent_card.description: The emberdeck intent card produced by this design. emberdeck (ED) is MCP-backed and degradable; when ED is unavailable, this is the Omitted branch (the design still stands but the card was not created), NOT a fabricated card id. Measured.value carries the created card's RowRef-style id.

adr.$comment: R34 — Architecture Decision Record is an M1 FIELD (standalone prose rule deleted; shape forced by grammar). Required in Design.
adr.description: R34 ARCHITECTURE DECISION RECORD (M1 field — R34 standalone prose rule deleted, the shape is now grammar-forced). Records the architectural decision: title, context, decision, consequences, status, optional considered alternatives.

cross_verify_required.$comment: R5 floor — Design mode is HIGH-STAKES, so the cross-verify trigger is carried here. Mitigation, not elimination.
cross_verify_required.description: R5 CROSS-VERIFY TRIGGER (irreducible-floor mitigation). Design mode is high-stakes, so Decide carries the multi-model/pyreez cross-verification trigger. Mitigates self-asserted-truth / semantic-boundary residuals; does NOT eliminate them.

cross_verify_result.description: Required in Design; not_run when pyreez did not run (or cross_verify_required==false). Always recorded in Design mode (single producing rule, decide-reviewer '반드시 기록'). 'agree' = pyreez ran and matched; 'disagree' = pyreez ran and conflicts (self-misjudgment signal → failure_origin=verify escalate); 'inconclusive' = ran but undetermined; 'not_run' = pyreez absent/error/timeout or not triggered.

task_list.$comment: §10 task model — Design MAY decompose into tasks. R19/R33 ParallelMarker: same-file tasks => parallel_marker=false is an M2 cross-task check, not a grammar keyword. affected count is a CountClaim (R23).
task_list.description: Optional task decomposition for the design (§10 task model). Each task carries R19/R33 parallel_marker (M2 validator: tasks touching the same file MUST both be parallel_marker=false) and an affected_files_count that is a CountClaim (R23 — never a bare integer for a measured count).

parallel_marker.$comment: R19/R33 — same-file tasks => parallel_marker=false is an M2 cross-task check.
parallel_marker.description: R19/R33: may this task run in parallel with siblings. M2 validator: any two tasks sharing a file path MUST both be false (mutual exclusion over file paths).

files.description: File paths this task touches (read input paths, not §5 cross-step refs). Drives the same-file parallel_marker M2 check.

affected_files_count.$comment: R23 — a count of measured things MUST be a CountClaim, never a bare integer.
affected_files_count.description: R23 count-type: number of files this task affects, as a CountClaim {value, source:{command, raw_stdout_sha256}}. M2 validator re-derives value from raw_stdout.

compound_top_level.$comment: Compound TOP-LEVEL discriminant (Design-only). Trigger field for reviewer/compound-toplevel-trigger: orchestrator carries the flow_type=compound top-level marker into the decision output. true => gate_rules + sub_flow_sequence both required (M2). Optional/absent => not a compound top-level Design (both may be omitted).
compound_top_level.description: Compound top-level marker. true when this Design is the TOP-LEVEL of a Compound flow (flow_type=compound, owns sub-flow decomposition); orchestrator sets it from the flow_type. When true, gate_rules AND sub_flow_sequence are both required (M2 reviewer/compound-toplevel-trigger). When absent/false, this is a non-compound Design and both are optional.

gate_rules.$comment: Compound TOP-LEVEL only. JsonLogic predicate + action enum. Gate executor = orchestrator code (NOT LLM). M2: var paths must exist in sub_flow output structure.
gate_rules.description: Compound top-level ONLY (compound-recursion): gate criteria evaluated by the orchestrator (NOT the LLM) BETWEEN sub-flows. Each rule = a JsonLogic condition predicate + an action enum (proceed|pivot|abort|retry). M2 validator: JsonLogic grammar validity + every `var` path exists in the real sub_flow output structure. pivot cap 2/compound, retry cap 1/sub_flow.

condition.$comment: JsonLogic predicate object (borrowed mini-DSL; no new DSL). Free-form object; M2 validates JsonLogic grammar + var-path existence.
condition.description: A JsonLogic condition predicate ({ operator: [operands...] }). Allowed operators: ==, !=, >, <, >=, <=, and, or, not, in, var. Shape is an open object here; M2 validator checks JsonLogic validity and that every var path resolves in the sub_flow context.

action.description: Gate action taken when condition holds.

sub_flow_sequence.$comment: Compound TOP-LEVEL only. Decide(Design) defines the sub-flow order (decomposition + ordering). Each entry references a sub-flow by RowRef.
sub_flow_sequence.description: Compound top-level ONLY (compound-recursion): the ordered list of sub-flows this Compound decomposes into (Decide-Design owns decomposition + ordering; Triage does NOT). Each entry: a sub-flow ref (RowRef), its flow type, and the prior sub-flow refs it depends on. Reviewer: Compound top-level requires gate_rules + sub_flow_sequence both defined.

sub_flow_ref.description: RowRef to the sub-flow row (§5 row id, never a file path).

depends_on.description: RowRefs of prior sub-flows this one depends on (ordering).

oneOf.$comment: MODE = record. Self-evident decision. decision_record + reason carry the 1-pair the reviewer requires; no plan/design payload. All plan/design-only props forbidden (false) so grammar cannot emit a record carrying them.
MODE = plan. Reviewer: options N>=2 compared + chosen with reason + ordering. Design-only props forbidden.

MODE = design. Full design: design_document(architecture+policy+userflow+req) + intent_card + ADR(R34) + cross_verify(R5) required; options_deliberated (Measured|Omitted, always present per R22 degrade) + cross_verify_result (always recorded; not_run when pyreez not run) also required; optional task_list + Compound gate_rules/sub_flow_sequence. Plan/Record-only props forbidden.
BRANCH B — reclassify_required. Investigate surfaced triage_mismatch; Decide does NOT proceed. Special output. (Triage reclassify cap 3/flow.)

reclassify.description: Reclassify signal: Investigate surfaced a triage_mismatch. Decide produces this special output and does NOT proceed; orchestrator re-enters Triage as a new flow type (reclassify cap 3/flow). Carries the mismatch reason + provenance.

suggested_flow_type.description: Optional advisory new flow type for Triage to consider (advisory only; Triage re-decides).

triage_mismatch_ref.description: RowRef to the Investigate row that surfaced the triage_mismatch (§5 row id).

based_on.$comment: reclassify rests on the Investigate row that surfaced triage_mismatch only — Ground/Triage were not yet consumed for a decision. The local properties list ONLY investigate_ref + additionalProperties:false + required:[investigate_ref] closes this to EXACTLY one ref, matching prose 'based_on:{investigate_ref} only'; an empty based_on:{} is forbidden (investigate_ref required) and extra refs (ground_ref/triage_ref/...) are rejected. NO allOf:[BasedOn] here: BasedOn imports ground_ref/triage_ref/... as legal optional props, which under 2020-12 are NOT siblings of this object's additionalProperties (so they would be rejected as additional anyway) — importing it would be dead weight that misleadingly suggests those refs are permitted. The single-ref closure is self-evident from the local properties alone.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH C — request_upstream_deepen. Ground/Investigate too shallow to decide. Signal; orchestrator re-invokes that step with depth=deep (cap 1/flow). Then Verify is the final safety.

deepen_request.description: Upstream deepen signal: Decide cannot decide on shallow Ground/Investigate output. Orchestrator re-invokes the named step with depth=deep (cycle cap 1/flow; if still insufficient, Verify is the final safety with failure_origin=ground|investigate). Carries which step + why + provenance.

target_step.$comment: Which upstream step to deepen. Constrained to the two Decide can deepen (Ground / Investigate) — the shared StepName enum is wider, so this is a tightened local enum.
target_step.description: The upstream step to re-invoke with depth=deep. Only Ground or Investigate (the two Decide rests on).

missing_evidence.description: What specific evidence/facts are missing such that Decide cannot decide.

based_on.$comment: Decide always rests on Investigate AND Ground (Inputs); a deepen request names both upstreams it could not decide on, so both refs are required — an empty based_on:{} is forbidden.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH D — precondition_fault. The Input-preconditions gate (P8 garbage-in defense) found a required upstream field absent/malformed/enum-invalid/ref-unresolved BEFORE the decision activity. Decide does NOT decide; it escalates. Exclusive with the success/control branches: no `decision`/mode payload, no reclassify/deepen payload. failure_origin is the concrete broken upstream (ground|investigate). The 5-累積 halt cap (failure-routing.md) bounds producer⇄reviewer re-entry.

failure_origin.$comment: Tightened local enum over the shared FailureOrigin (the wider _defs enum carries verify/cap_exceeded/multiple which Decide's precondition gate cannot attribute). Only the two upstream steps Decide rests on can fault its preconditions.
failure_origin.description: Which upstream step's required field was absent/malformed. Decide's precondition gate can only attribute to ground or investigate (the two upstreams it reads). allOf-restricts the shared FailureOrigin enum.

faulting_field.description: The specific required upstream field that was absent/malformed/enum-invalid/ref-unresolved (e.g. 'compatibility_verdict.result', 'risk_surface', 'compatibility_verdict.partial_scope_handling.blocked_set' (when result==partial_proceed), 'compatibility_verdict.issues[].severity', 'risk_surface[].severity', 'investigate.based_on_ground').

cause.$comment: Closed enum over the FOUR authoritative mechanical causes the P8 Input-preconditions gate detects (decide.md Input preconditions + failure table). The taxonomy is structural-only (missing | type-mismatch | enum-invalid | ref-unresolved) — NOT a truth/semantic judgment. `detail` below is the free-text elaboration of THIS cause; grammar pins the cause to one of the four so a producer cannot emit an off-taxonomy cause.
cause.description: Which of the four mechanical precondition causes failed: missing (field absent) | type-mismatch (wrong type / not array etc.) | enum-invalid (value outside the field's enum, incl. a malformed issues[].severity / risk_surface[].severity item) | ref-unresolved (a RowRef did not resolve). Structural decision only — Decide does not judge truth/meaning (that is Verify's job).

detail.description: Free-text elaboration of the `cause` above: which precondition failed and how. NOT a truth/semantic judgment — only structural description (the closed taxonomy lives in `cause`).

based_on.$comment: Decide always rests on Investigate AND Ground; a precondition_fault must name both upstream rows it gated (failure_origin says which one faulted, but provenance still points at both reads), so an empty based_on:{} is forbidden.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH E — self_misjudgment. Design high-stakes cross-verify RAN and CONFLICTED (pyreez cross_verify_result==disagree). This is NOT a degrade (absence) — it is an opposite signal, so Decide does NOT emit a decision; it escalates with failure_origin=verify (the self-misjudgment route the prose mandates). Exclusive with the success/control branches: no `decision`/mode payload. A `decision` output carrying cross_verify_result=disagree is forbidden (M2 control/disagree-escalate) — disagree's ONLY home is here. The 5-累積 halt cap bounds re-entry.

failure_origin.$comment: Tightened to the single value `verify` — the disagree self-misjudgment is attributed to Verify (self-route, NO auto-reinvoke per _defs FailureOrigin). allOf-restricts the shared FailureOrigin enum to one member.
failure_origin.description: Always `verify`: Design cross-verify disagree is a self-misjudgment signal routed to Verify (failure-routing). allOf-restricts the shared FailureOrigin enum to the single value Decide's disagree-escalate can attribute.

cross_verify_result.$comment: Pinned to disagree — this branch EXISTS to carry the disagree case out of the `decision` branch. Same enum value as the Design-mode cross_verify_result field; here it is constrained to the conflicting value only.
cross_verify_result.description: Always `disagree`: pyreez cross-verification ran and conflicted with the decision. This is the only result value that reaches this branch; agree/inconclusive/not_run stay on the `decision` (design) branch.

detail.description: Why the cross-verification conflicts: what pyreez asserted vs what Decide concluded. Carries the self-misjudgment evidence for Verify.

based_on.$comment: Decide always rests on Investigate AND Ground; a self_misjudgment (Design cross-verify disagree) still names both upstream rows the decision was built on, so an empty based_on:{} is forbidden.

x-validator-contract.check: M2 REQUIRED: orchestrator computes the FORCED mode from Investigate schema fields using the SINGLE AUTHORITATIVE force definition F1-F4 in decide.md §'Mode 결정 방식 / 3. Mechanical force (R6)' + the precedence/tiebreak in §4-§5 (this validator does NOT re-define the numeric thresholds — it reads them from that single authority to avoid the dual-definition drift the prose closed for the issues>=2 case). Assert emitted `mode` == computed forced mode (F4 architecture->design supersedes F1/F2/F3->plan per depth-monotone precedence; absent any force, declared base mode subject to the §5 upgrade_pressure tiebreak). Mode upgrade triggers evaluated ONLY when compatibility_verdict.result in {proceed, partial_proceed}; otherwise Decide must not have executed at all. Cross-schema dependency: the Investigate field names this force reads (compatibility_verdict.issues[].severity, risk_surface[].severity, impact_map.affected_files_count, architecture_impact.has_architecture_level/public_api_changes) are owned by Investigate's schema — if those names change this validator must follow. ORDERING GUARANTEE: the P8 Input-preconditions gate (x-validator-contract control/precondition-fault) MUST pass BEFORE this R6 mode-force is computed — F1/F2/§5 evaluate count(... where severity in {...}) and public_api_changes.length only AFTER the gate has asserted each issues[]/risk_surface[] item severity is well-formed and public_api_changes is a well-formed array (decide.md §5 'F1/F2 및 §5 tiebreak가 ... 평가하기 전에'). Without this ordering a malformed item could silently drop from the `where` predicate (contributing 0) and lower the forced mode; the gate routes such garbage to precondition_fault first.
M2: assert depth agrees with mode (record->shallow; plan/design->deep).
M2 (folded into R27): assert declared_next_step == orchestrator-injected expected_next_step. Mismatch = chain divergence.
M2: every mode has decision_record present AND reason grounded in actual Ground/Investigate rows (reason.grounded_in / reason.source_tool resolve to real upstream rows).
M2: options_considered.length >= 2 (already grammar-enforced) AND chosen.option_id references an id present in options_considered.
M2: every depends_on id exists among sequencing[].step and the dependency graph is acyclic.

M2 REQUIRED where present: re-execute verify_probe.command, compare to expected_result. Self-assertion is not truth. OUTCOME ON MISMATCH: if re-execution does NOT match expected_result, the option's premise is falsified — the decide-reviewer REJECTS the `decision` (producer⇄reviewer re-entry, bounded by the 5-累積 (flow_id, step) halt cap). This is NOT precondition_fault (the probe is Decide's own self-assertion, not a malformed upstream field) and NOT self_misjudgment (that branch is reserved for Design pyreez cross_verify_result==disagree). A falsified verify_probe is a reviewer-reject; the producer re-decides (e.g. drops/replaces the falsified option or corrects expected_result).

M2 (Design high-stakes): if cross_verify_required=true, the cross-verification (pyreez/multi-model) must actually run; cross_verify_result=disagree is the self-misjudgment signal routed to Verify via result=self_misjudgment (NOT a `decision` output — see control/disagree-escalate).
M1 field (shape grammar-forced). No M2 truth check beyond presence in Design mode; ADR content is design prose.
M2 REQUIRED: re-hash CountClaim.source.raw_stdout, confirm raw_stdout_sha256, re-derive value. Bare integer is forbidden by grammar (CountClaim shape).
M2 REQUIRED: any two tasks whose `files` arrays share a path MUST both have parallel_marker=false (mutual exclusion over file paths).
M2: every depends_on id exists among task_list[].id and the graph is acyclic.
M2: dedup by (type, scope_hash); reject duplicate (type, scope_hash) pairs. Followups are QUEUEd by orchestrator, not auto-run.

M2: JsonLogic grammar validity (operators in {==,!=,>,<,>=,<=,and,or,not,in,var}); every `var` path resolves in the real sub_flow output context. Gate executor is orchestrator code, not the LLM. pivot cap 2/compound, retry cap 1/sub_flow.
M2: a Compound TOP-LEVEL Design must define BOTH gate_rules and sub_flow_sequence; sub_flow_sequence depends_on refs must resolve and be acyclic.

Floor (partly M2): every fact/decision/finding item carries a source_tool; provenance presence is grammar-forced, that the named origin truly produced the value is mitigated by cross-verification, not eliminated.
Floor, KEEP polarity: unverified is designed to persist to Verify (the single gate). NO 'no unverified remain' forbid. Whether unverified:false is honest is self-asserted-truth residual.

M2 (decide-reviewer 'unverified 정합성'): mode-conditioned polarity. Record/Plan are not cross-verify targets => unverified MUST be false. Design: unverified true iff cross_verify_result in {not_run, inconclusive} (pyreez degrade), false when agree. cross_verify_result=disagree never reaches a `decision` output (it routes to result=self_misjudgment with failure_origin=verify — see control/disagree-escalate); a `decision` carrying cross_verify_result=disagree is rejected, so disagree has no unverified polarity to define here. This is a TRUTH/polarity check (not grammar — R13 KEEP-polarity must not forbid true); reject polarity violations.

M2/gate: the Input-preconditions gate detected a required upstream field absent/malformed/enum-invalid/ref-unresolved; `cause` is pinned (grammar) to one of {missing, type-mismatch, enum-invalid, ref-unresolved} and `detail` is its free elaboration; failure_origin in {ground, investigate} names the concrete broken upstream. ITEM-LEVEL well-formedness: the gate also asserts each compatibility_verdict.issues[] item carries severity in {fatal,high,medium,low} (_defs.SeverityIssue) and each risk_surface[] item carries severity in {low,med,high,critical} (_defs.Severity) BEFORE F1/F2/§5 evaluate count(... where severity in {...}); a non-empty array with a malformed/enum-invalid item severity escalates here (cause=enum-invalid|missing) instead of silently failing the `where` predicate (contributing 0) and lowering the forced mode. Decide does NOT decide on this branch. 5-累積 (flow_id, step) halt cap bounds producer⇄reviewer re-entry (failure-routing.md).

M2/governor: Triage reclassify cap 3/flow; Decide does not proceed on this branch. Also assert reclassify.triage_mismatch_ref (grammar-required) resolves to the Investigate row that actually surfaced the triage_mismatch (reviewer/'reclassify_required는 Investigate.triage_mismatch가 실재할 때만') — an unresolved/stale ref is rejected.
M2/governor: upstream deepen cap 1/flow; after that Verify is the final safety (failure_origin=ground|investigate forces reclassify with depth=deep).

M2: a `decision` output (any mode) carrying cross_verify_result=disagree is REJECTED — Design cross-verify disagree must be emitted as result=self_misjudgment with failure_origin=verify and cross_verify_result=disagree (the only branch that carries disagree). agree/inconclusive/not_run stay on the `decision` (design) branch. Bounds: 5-累積 (flow_id, step) halt cap bounds producer⇄reviewer re-entry.

M2: when result=decision AND upstream Investigate compatibility_verdict.result==partial_proceed AND partial_scope_handling.blocked_set is non-empty, followup_flows MUST cover every blocked scope (each blocked_set entry's scope_hash appears among the source=blocked_scope followup_flows[].scope_hash). The join counts ONLY entries whose `source`==blocked_scope — audit-sourced entries are excluded, so a scope_hash collision between an audit finding and a blocked scope can neither spuriously satisfy coverage nor conflate a real blocked scope. JOIN KEY: blocked_set[].scope_hash -> followup_flows[source=blocked_scope][].scope_hash — the upstream blocked_set entry shape is { scope_ref, scope_hash } (declared + P8-asserted well-formed, see below), so the coverage join trusts a DECLARED member, never an undeclared field. Missing coverage => reject (required-field-missing); a partial completion must not terminate without queuing its blocked followups. PRECONDITION: this check TRUSTS partial_scope_handling.{proceed_set,blocked_set} to be present + well-formed AND each blocked_set entry to carry { scope_ref, scope_hash } — that well-formedness (incl. the scope_hash member the join reads) is asserted BEFORE this validator by the P8 Input-preconditions gate (decide.md Input preconditions table: partial_scope_handling required + each blocked_set entry well-formed {scope_ref, scope_hash} when result==partial_proceed), so a stale/missing/scope_hash-less blocked_set entry escalates as precondition_fault rather than silently skipping required followups or crashing here.

M2 trigger field: when decision.mode==design AND decision.compound_top_level==true, the design IS a Compound TOP-LEVEL Design and MUST define BOTH gate_rules and sub_flow_sequence; when compound_top_level is absent/false, both may be omitted. compound_top_level is the discriminant the orchestrator carries into the decision output (derived from the flow_type=compound top-level marker) so this conditional-required rule has a mechanical trigger.
